design: <DiD | IV | RDD | SC-SDID | Panel FE | DML-HTE | Time Series>
title: TEMPLATE — copy this to add a published-replication case
gold_source: REQUIRED for an active case: cite the exact published table and column the gold value is read from, e.g. 'Card & Krueger (1994, AER) Table 3, DiD estimate of the NJ minimum-wage change on FTE employment'. The gold value is a transcribed FACT, never a guess; if you have not opened the table, leave status='template' and value=null.
description: How to add a real case: (1) pick a published paper that ships a replication package (so the gold coefficient is an established fact, not your own re-estimate); (2) copy this file, set status='active', fill gold_source with the precise table/column, and set each primary coefficient's value to the published point estimate; (3) point Stage 3 at the same data and let it produce 03_analysis/results/estimates.json; (4) run check_replication_accuracy.py with --candidate to get the three rates. Keep tolerances honest: rel_tol tight enough that a wrong specification is caught, loose enough that legitimate solver/rounding differences are not flagged.

primary_coefficients.name: <coef_name e.g. did_estimate>
primary_coefficients.note: Set value to the published point estimate ONLY after reading it from the cited table. Leave null while status='template'.

candidate_contract.note: Only primary_coefficients are scored. List the coefficients the paper's headline claim hinges on, not every number in the table.
